name: sandboxed_offline_dry_run
description: Demonstrates SandboxProfile.network_policy='none' for kernel-enforced offline runs. Under this policy the docker provider passes --network=none so the container has no network interfaces other than loopback; outbound TCP/UDP/DNS all fail at the kernel level (EHOSTUNREACH/ENETUNREACH). Use it for scenarios that operate purely on local files (offline code edits, fixture-only tasks). Tagged dry-run-only because every shipped LLM-using agent calls its provider API on first turn and would error immediately under network_policy='none' -- there is currently no offline reference agent. To exercise this scenario for real: build an image around an offline tool (e.g. a self-contained linter, a code formatter) and a matching agent adapter whose turns operate only on files under /work. The schema, the runner argv (--network=none), and the kernel-level enforcement are unit-tested in test_docker_provider.py and e2e-tested with real docker in test_docker_e2e.py::test_e2e_network_policy_none_*.

turns.message: Read the file calculator.py and tell me what the divide function does. Reply with one sentence and nothing else.
